scenario: Rails work session - audit the query Relation contract before a rework

map_quality.rationale: The answer clearly identifies Rails as a multi-gem monorepo, pins the lazy query object as ActiveRecord::Relation in activerecord/lib/active_record/, names the creation entry points (core.rb:442, scoping/named.rb:22/:154, querying.rb:24), the relation/ module subdir, and the subclass hierarchy — enough for the agent to pick an entry point with only targeted follow-ups. Cross-gem dependents and scattered activerecord/lib call sites are absent, but that is step 4's job.

specificity.rationale: File paths are concrete and annotated: relation.rb (1539 lines), relation/query_methods.rb, spawn_methods.rb, delegation.rb, merger.rb, etc., plus line numbers on creation entry points (core.rb:442, scoping/named.rb:22/:154, querying.rb:24). The subclass list (association_relation.rb, collection_proxy.rb) is exact.

justification.rationale: The answer labels Relation as 'lazy, chainable' and notes @values storage and exec_queries/to_sql execution, and spawn_methods as 'clone/merge', but never explains WHY execution is deferred (lazy until enumeration) or WHY the query interface is split into mixed-in modules rather than a monolith — the architectural reasoning is absent.

uncertainty.rationale: No uncertainty is flagged anywhere; the answer presents all paths and line numbers as definitive without noting which modules it did not open or where the connection is inferred rather than verified, which could lead the agent to over-trust the map on unexplored edges.

relationship.rationale: The answer states that modules in relation/ are 'mixed into' Relation, that spawn_methods.rb handles 'clone/merge', that delegation.rb does 'per-model subclassing + method delegation', and that querying.rb 'delegates ~90 class methods to all' — these are connecting relationships, not just file names. Missing is an explicit statement of the finder/scope → spawn relation → merge → execute chain as a sequence.

steps.step: Map the Relation contract and what it is built on

map_quality.rationale: The answer maps every major contract piece with file:line: the full includes list at relation.rb:68-70, the VALUE_METHODS constants at :54-66 with the note that 'the merger and assert_valid_keys depend on them', the initialize signature at :78-96, and each module (query_methods, finder_methods, calculations, spawn_methods, delegation, merger) with specific line anchors; an agent can pick any of these entry points and verify with small targeted reads.

specificity.rationale: All four rubric-named files (relation/query_methods.rb, relation/finder_methods.rb, relation/spawn_methods.rb, relation/delegation.rb) appear by exact path with line numbers; merger.rb and calculations.rb are also named; no vague 'query layer' stand-ins remain.

justification.rationale: The answer provides category labels ('Query building / chaining', 'Spawning/merging') and notes that VALUE_METHODS constants are load-bearing for the merger, but it does not explain WHY the interface is composed from focused modules, WHY chaining spawns a fresh copy rather than mutating in place, or WHY delegation routes undefined methods to the model class; the structure is described without the design rationale.

uncertainty.rationale: No uncertainty flags appear: every line number is stated with full confidence and no caveats about modules not opened (Batches, Explain) or delegation paths not verified; an agent would act on this without a prompt to double-check.

relationship.rationale: The answer states the binding mechanism for the core modules ('Includes … FinderMethods, Calculations, SpawnMethods, QueryMethods') and labels roles explicitly ('Spawning/merging: spawn_methods.rb', 'Delegation/per-model subclassing: delegation.rb → DelegateCache, ClassMethods.create'), but it does not trace HOW spawn clones the relation on each chain call or HOW DelegateCache routes undefined methods to the model class; the connecting paths are implied by category labels rather than written as an explicit A → B → X chain.

steps.step: Trace how a query is built up and executed

map_quality.rationale: The answer traces the full path in numbered order — Create → Chain/spawn → Merge → Compile → Execute — with concrete file:line at every layer (spawn_methods.rb:9-10, merger.rb:17,58, relation.rb:1212/1436/1460/1492). An agent could pick an entry point and follow the chain with only small, targeted lookups to verify line numbers.

specificity.rationale: Each layer is named exactly: SpawnMethods#spawn at spawn_methods.rb:9-10, Relation::HashMerger/Merger at merger.rb:17,58, build_arel at query_methods.rb:1673/1828, exec_main_query at relation.rb:1460 — no vague 'the query code' language anywhere.

justification.rationale: The answer describes HOW spawn works ('dups @values and resets caches') but never states WHY chaining spawns a fresh relation (immutability of an un-executed relation) or WHY execution is deferred to load; it lists the call chain without the design rationale behind it.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer presents all five layers with no flags on anything unverified; it gives exact line numbers for merger internals and exec paths without noting which it opened vs. inferred, leaving the agent no signal on where to confirm.

relationship.rationale: The numbered 1–5 structure explicitly assembles the path as a chain — each step names what it receives from the prior step (e.g., spawn produces a clone that merge! then routes through Merger, which feeds build_arel, which feeds exec_main_query) — rather than listing files in isolation.

steps.step: Audit every dependent of the Relation contract

map_quality.rationale: The answer covers every area the rubric names — connection_adapters database_statements, sanitization.rb `===` test, integration.rb cache key, encryption extended_deterministic_queries, signed_id/token_for RelationMethods, join_dependency, scoping/named — plus both cross-gem dependents (enumerable.rb:145/:161 and query_command.rb:174-182), each with file:line and a usage phrase; the scattered fan-out an agent needs to act safely is mapped.

specificity.rationale: Virtually every entry carries an exact file:line pair (e.g. `sanitization.rb:214`, `encryption/extended_deterministic_queries.rb:44`, `railties/.../query_command.rb:174-182`, `activesupport/.../enumerable.rb:145`) plus one concrete phrase; no area is left as 'various query helpers.'

justification.rationale: Grouping by area and per-entry phrases explain what each dependent does (e.g. 'inlines the relation's SQL as a bind/subquery,' 'rewrites where-values for deterministic encryption,' 'core extensions that mirror Relation#pluck/#pick so arrays and relations stay interchangeable'); the WHY each is at risk from a contract change is implied by the group headings rather than stated explicitly for each entry.

uncertainty.rationale: The enumerable entry flags 'no class reference — found only by name parity,' and the `===` type tests are surfaced explicitly, but the answer never broadly states that a class-name grep would miss these categories or that the short-name call sites in the query layer itself are unreachable by FQN search; the uncertainty acknowledgment is partial.

relationship.rationale: For most dependents the connecting path is explicit: `is_a?(Relation)` / `ActiveRecord::Relation === value` type tests (sanitization, spawn_methods, query_methods), `< Relation` subclassing (AssociationRelation, CollectionProxy), `RelationMethods` mixin extension (signed_id, token_for), `case result when ActiveRecord::Relation` for the renderer, and 'name parity' (not a reference) for enumerable — the HOW is stated rather than a bare file list.

steps.step: Trace how relation behavior is reused for associations

map_quality.rationale: The answer gives file:line for both subclassing paths (AssociationRelation < Relation at association_relation.rb:4, CollectionProxy < Relation at collection_proxy.rb:31), the query-build site (association.rb:107-115 via target_scope.merge!(association_scope)), AssociationScope at association_scope.rb:21/:124, the no-join variant, and the delegation glue at relation/delegation.rb:7-14 — an agent could act on this with only small follow-up reads.

specificity.rationale: Every piece is named with a concrete file and line range: association_relation.rb:4-5/:10/:31-39/:43, collection_proxy.rb:31, association.rb:107-115/:315/:110-111, association_scope.rb:21/:124, delegation.rb:7-14 — no vague 'association code' references.

justification.rationale: The answer states the subclassing fact ('An association collection *is* a relation by subclassing') and notes 'the user-facing posts.comments object', but never explains WHY this design exists — that subclassing Relation gives the collection the full chainable query interface automatically, so every scope method works on associations without reimplementation.

uncertainty.rationale: No uncertainty flags anywhere — the answer states CollectionProxy line numbers, delegation details, and scope-build sites with full confidence but never notes which methods or delegation paths it didn't open or verify, leaving the agent with no signal about where to double-check.

relationship.rationale: The connecting path is implicit but traceable: CollectionProxy/AssociationRelation subclass Relation → AssociationScope adds constraints via association.rb:110's merge → spawn reuses the spawn contract; the delegation.rb list ties all four proxy classes to the delegate mechanism. The chain is not written as an explicit A→B→C sentence but the named relationships (< Relation, merge!, spawn, delegation list) let an agent assemble it.

steps.step: Assess the blast radius of the contract change

map_quality.rationale: The answer covers most of the required scattered call sites — sanitization's type test, integration cache key, encryption, signed_id, railties query_command, activesupport enumerable — with file:line and a risk ranking, but notably omits connection_handling (role/shard execution) and scoping/named (named-scope definition), both called out as key unqualified call sites in the rubric; join_dependency appears only indirectly via join_association.rb rather than the join_dependency.rb entry point.

specificity.rationale: Almost every dependent is cited by concrete file and line (e.g., `sanitization.rb:214`, `spawn_methods.rb:9,43-53`, `encryption/extended_deterministic_queries.rb:44`, `railties .../query_command.rb:174`) with a one-phrase reason; the only vagueness is `token_for.rb:38` which is named but not explained, and the two missing files weigh against completeness.

justification.rationale: The answer explains the WHY for each tier — delegation breaks every model's relation class, type tests 'silently drop subqueries', cross-gem enumerable drifts if method semantics change — rather than just listing files; the distinction between spawn/merge (corrupt all chaining) vs cache keys (correctness only) vs cross-gem render (easy to forget) is actionable reasoning the agent can act on.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer flags nothing as uncertain or unverified; the parenthetical '(easy to forget — not in activerecord)' on cross-gem items is a reminder, not a confidence qualifier, and there is no acknowledgment that unqualified short-name references or additional gem-boundary callers may have been missed by any static pass.

relationship.rationale: Most dependents have an explicit connecting relationship stated — 'per-model subclassing via Relation.create', 'case when ActiveRecord::Relation + .to_sql/.offset/.limit', 'type tests … silently drops subqueries', 'mixed into the contract' — so the agent can see the path, not just the file; the two missing dependents (connection_handling, scoping/named) leave their relationship paths unstated, and the join_dependency chain is incomplete.

map_quality.rationale: The map covers every required layer — relation.rb with line ranges, all six query modules, dependents grouped by area (type-tests, construction, execution, subclasses, features, cross-gem), and a full test list including railties/test/commands/query_command_test.rb and activesupport/test/core_ext/enumerable_test.rb — with file:line throughout; a teammate could land the change from this map, with only small confirmatory lookups for fixture specifics.

specificity.rationale: Files are named to the line (sanitization.rb:214, query_methods.rb:1762/1782, database_statements.rb:803, enumerable.rb:145/161, query_command.rb:174-182) rather than described vaguely; insertion points like relation.rb:78/1212/1460 and spawn_methods.rb:9/43-53 are concrete enough to navigate directly.

justification.rationale: Area groupings (type_tests_spawn_merge, sql_bind_handling, eager_load_joins, cross_gem) and the blast_radius_ranked section explain WHY each cluster is at risk; the notes section explains WHY short-name and type-test dependents escape a grep — but per-file justification within each JSON array is implicit from the category label rather than stated per entry.

uncertainty.rationale: The notes block explicitly flags that a class-name grep misses short-name (`is_a?`/`===`/`.arel`/method-mirroring) dependents, which is the main blind spot; however, uncertainty is collected at the end rather than flagged inline per item, so the agent must remember to carry it forward when reviewing individual files.

relationship.rationale: The creation_and_execution_order block writes the chain explicitly (querying.rb delegate → core.rb Relation.create → spawn → merger → arel → exec), and blast_radius_ranked names the connecting mechanism per tier (per-model subclassing, spawn/merge, arel/exec, type-test/bind, cache-key, cross-gem mirror/render); however, the test list is not connected to which execution path each test exercises, leaving that link implicit.
